title: New York - New Jersey Job Recovery Expected to Continue in 1998

source.name: Federal Reserve Bank of New York

summary: The New York Fed published a regional employment forecast predicting continued job recovery in the New York-New Jersey region for 1998, with overall employment expected to rise 1.3 percent or approximately 149,000 new jobs. The authors projected that employment gains in New York State, New York City, and New Jersey would be smaller than in 1997, while noting that Asian market turmoil would have a minor negative impact and that a WTO financial services agreement would likely improve regional employment prospects.
key_points: Regional employment recovery expected to continue in 1998 with 1.3% growth (~149,000 new jobs)
New York State, New York City, and New Jersey each expected to record employment gains
Employment gains projected to be smaller in 1998 than in 1997
Asian market turmoil expected to have minor negative impact on exported goods
WTO financial services trade liberalization agreement projected to improve regional employment prospects
Authors are James Orr and Rae D. Rosen, senior economists at the New York Fed
why_it_matters: Source content concerns a regional employment forecast for the New York-New Jersey area from 1998, with no discussion of market structure, trading operations, clearing/settlement mechanisms, or contemporary macro catalysts relevant to market structure research.
